q.en: What is the main focus of this book?
q.zh: 这本书的核心内容是什么？

options.en: The challenges and rewards of thru-hiking the Appalachian Trail
options.zh: 纵走阿巴拉契亚小径所面临的挑战与收获

options.en: How to build a canoe from scratch
options.zh: 如何从零开始制作独木舟

options.en: The history of the U.S. space program
options.zh: 美国太空计划的历史

options.en: A fictional tale about a talking bear
options.zh: 一个会说话的熊的虚构故事

explain.en: Ben Gaddes describes his 2016 thru-hike of the A.T., focusing on both its difficulties and its rewards.
explain.zh: 本·加德斯讲述了自己2016年纵走阿巴拉契亚小径的经历，重点在于其中的困难与收获。

q.en: Which statement about the Appalachian Trail is true according to the book?
q.zh: 根据本书，关于阿巴拉契亚小径的哪一项是正确的？

options.en: It is about 2,190 miles long, runs through 14 states, and is the world's longest hiking-only footpath
options.zh: 它长约2190英里，横跨14个州，是世界上最长的仅供徒步的小径

options.en: It is only 100 miles long and located in one state
options.zh: 它只有100英里长，位于一个州内

options.en: It is the world's longest road for cars
options.zh: 它是世界上最长的汽车公路

options.en: It was completed in the year 2000
options.zh: 它于2000年建成

explain.en: Completed in 1937, the A.T. spans about 2,190 miles across 14 states and remains the longest hiking-only footpath.
explain.zh: 小径1937年建成，全长约2190英里，横跨14个州，至今仍是最长的仅供徒步的小径。

q.en: When did Ben start and finish his thru-hike?
q.zh: 本的纵走是在何时开始、何时结束的？

options.en: He started at Springer Mountain, Georgia, on April 4, 2016, and summitted Mount Katahdin, Maine, on October 1, 2016
options.zh: 他2016年4月4日从佐治亚州斯普林杰山出发，2016年10月1日登顶缅因州卡塔丁山

options.en: He started in 2000 and finished in 2001
options.zh: 他2000年开始，2001年结束

options.en: He started and finished in the same weekend
options.zh: 他在同一个周末开始并结束

options.en: He never actually finished the trail
options.zh: 他实际上从未走完

explain.en: Ben took his first step at Springer Mountain on April 4, 2016, and reached Mount Katahdin on October 1, 2016, after nearly six months.
explain.zh: 本于2016年4月4日从斯普林杰山迈出第一步，经过近六个月，于2016年10月1日抵达卡塔丁山。

q.en: How did Ben prepare for his thru-hike?
q.zh: 本为纵走做了哪些准备？

options.en: He spent months walking with a heavy pack, worked at an outdoor store, and practiced setting up his tent and crossing the Brooklyn Bridge
options.zh: 他用数月背着沉重的背包行走，在户外店打工，并练习搭帐篷和走布鲁克林大桥

options.en: He did nothing and left the same day he decided to go
options.zh: 他什么都没做，决定去就当天出发

options.en: He hired a personal trainer to lift weights only
options.zh: 他只请了私人教练举重

options.en: He flew over the trail in a helicopter
options.zh: 他乘坐直升机飞越小径

explain.en: Ben prepared intensively for months: hiking with a heavy pack, learning gear at an outdoor store, and practicing with his tent and pack.
explain.zh: 本进行了数月的密集准备：背着重包徒步、在户外店学习装备，并练习搭帐篷和负重行走。

q.en: The book describes a 'trail community' of hikers from many backgrounds. What does bringing these different people together help show?
q.zh: 书中描述了由不同背景的徒步者组成的“步道社群”。把这些人汇聚在一起说明了什么？

options.en: That people of all ages and backgrounds hike the A.T. for many personal reasons, such as recovering from tragedies
options.zh: 各个年龄和背景的人都为各自的原因（如从悲剧中恢复）来走阿巴拉契亚小径

options.en: That only professional athletes hike the trail
options.zh: 只有职业运动员才走这条小径

options.en: That everyone on the trail is the same age
options.zh: 小径上的人都同龄

options.en: That hikers are not allowed to talk to each other
options.zh: 徒步者之间不允许交谈

explain.en: The varied community shows the trail draws all kinds of people, many seeking healing, making it a shared human experience.
explain.zh: 多样的社群说明小径吸引各类人群，许多人为疗愈而来，使之成为共同的人类体验。

q.en: What was a typical daily routine for Ben on the trail?
q.zh: 本在小径上典型的每日作息是怎样的？

options.en: He woke with the Sun, hiked until reaching his daily goal, and camped before sunset, eating lightweight, non-spoiling food
options.zh: 他随日出醒来，走到当日目标后、日落前扎营，吃轻便且不易坏的食物

options.en: He slept until noon and hiked only at night
options.zh: 他睡到中午，只在夜里走

options.en: He ate at fancy restaurants every day
options.zh: 他每天在高级餐厅吃饭

options.en: He rode a bicycle instead of walking
options.zh: 他骑单车而不是步行

explain.en: Ben hiked daily from sunrise toward a mileage goal, camping before sunset and relying on light, easy-to-prepare foods.
explain.zh: 本每天从日出走到当日里程目标，日落前扎营，依靠轻便、易准备的食物。

q.en: What safety measures does Ben describe for staying safe on the trail?
q.zh: 本描述了哪些保障小径安全的措施？

options.en: He filtered all water, kept clean to avoid illness, stayed dry and warm to prevent hypothermia, and used bear poles for food
options.zh: 他过滤所有饮用水、保持清洁防生病、保持干暖以防失温，并用“熊杆”悬挂食物

options.en: He ignored all wild animals and drank from streams directly
options.zh: 他无视所有野兽，直接喝溪水

options.en: He traveled only during thunderstorms
options.zh: 他只在雷暴时行进

options.en: He left his food out to feed the bears
options.zh: 他把食物摆出来喂熊

explain.en: Ben filtered water, minded hygiene, prevented hypothermia by staying dry/warm, and stored food on bear poles away from camp.
explain.zh: 本过滤饮水、注意卫生、保持干暖以防失温，并把有食物气味的东西挂在远离营地的“熊杆”上。

q.en: How does the author use the night his tent collapsed in a storm to make a point?
q.zh: 作者如何借暴风雨中帐篷坍塌的那一夜来表达观点？

options.en: He shows that a frightening, 'impossible' challenge can transform into something beautiful, like the amazing sunrise he then saw
options.zh: 他说明可怕、“不可能”的挑战也能转化为美好的事物，比如他随后看到的绝美日出

options.en: He shows that tents are useless and should never be used
options.zh: 他说明帐篷毫无用处，绝不该用

options.en: He shows that he gave up and went home
options.zh: 他说明自己放弃并回家了

options.en: He shows that the trail is too dangerous for anyone
options.zh: 他说明小径对任何人来说都太危险

explain.en: The collapsed-tent episode is structured as a turning point, illustrating how hardship can become a rewarding experience.
explain.zh: 帐篷坍塌事件是作为转折点来安排的，说明了困境如何能变成难忘的收获。

q.en: How did Ben feel about himself by the end of the hike?
q.zh: 走到终点时，本对自己的感受如何？

options.en: He felt stronger, more confident, and accomplished, and for the first time called himself an athlete
options.zh: 他感到更强壮、更自信、更有成就感，并第一次称自己为运动员

options.en: He felt weaker and regretted the whole trip
options.zh: 他感到更虚弱，并后悔整趟旅程

options.en: He felt exactly the same as when he started
options.zh: 他感觉和出发时一模一样

options.en: He decided he would never walk again
options.zh: 他决定再也不走路了

explain.en: On summiting Katahdin, Ben felt transformed—strong, confident, accomplished—and called himself an athlete for the first time.
explain.zh: 登顶卡塔丁山时，本觉得自己焕然一新——强壮、自信、有成就感，并首次自称运动员。

q.en: Why does Ben end the book by saying he hiked 'one step at a time' and that it was 'an invaluable experience'?
q.zh: 为什么本在结尾说自己“一步一步”地走，并称这是“无比珍贵的经历”？

options.en: To share the lesson that big dreams are reached step by step, and that the challenges made the achievement worthwhile
options.zh: 为了传达“宏大梦想靠一步步实现”，以及挑战让这份成就变得值得的感悟

options.en: To complain that the trail was a waste of time
options.zh: 为了抱怨走小径是浪费时间

options.en: To tell readers to avoid all long hikes
options.zh: 为了劝读者别去长途徒步

options.en: To brag that he finished faster than everyone
options.zh: 为了吹嘘自己比任何人都快完成

explain.en: Ben closes with the 'one step at a time' message to inspire readers and affirm that the struggle was worth the reward.
explain.zh: 本以“一步一步”作结，既鼓舞读者，也肯定这段艰辛终有回报。

prompt.en: Ben says he hiked the trail 'one step at a time' and that a challenge which seemed 'insurmountable' turned into something beautiful. Think of a big challenge in your own life (school, a skill, a fear). How could approaching it 'one step at a time' help you, and what might the reward be?
prompt.zh: 本说自己“一步一步”走完小径，而看似“无法逾越”的挑战最终变成了美好的事物。想想你生活中的一个大挑战（学业、某项技能、某种恐惧）。用“一步一步”的方式面对它会有什么帮助？回报可能是什么？

en: Break the challenge into small, manageable steps like Ben's daily mileage goals.
Imagine how you might feel after sticking with it, as Ben felt on Katahdin.
zh: 像本设定每日里程目标那样，把这个挑战拆成小而可行的步骤。
想象坚持下去后你可能的感受，正如本在卡塔丁山时的心情。
